input.content:
# Final Results - 331 Questions

## Performance Summary
- Total Predictions: 331 (all resolved)
- Accuracy: 11.5%
- Brier Skill Score: -0.021
- TW-Score: -869.35

## Top Wins (highest TW-Score gains)
- Japan Women's Asian Cup: +30.86
- Australia visa Iran ban: +30.94
- Jerusalem Old City explosion: +28.52
- Al Jazeera journalists surrounded: +27.69
- Pakistan IQAir most polluted: +26.00
- Kharg island bombing: +28.42
- Khiam mosque minaret: +21.68
- Israel Katz official confirmed: +20.56
- Mexico navy missing boats: +21.76
- UAE Afghanistan release: +14.95
- UAE Iran launch area: +13.94
- Kuwait Iranian raid plant: +12.77
- Ukraine drone Latvia: +9.95

## Top Losses  
- India condemning Pakistan: -36.92
- Eritrea match in Meknes: -26.32
- Germany Strait Hormuz refusal: -18.09
- Chuck Norris Hawaii: -17.48
- NGO Emergency Mediterranean: -16.42
- Iranian Red Crescent rescue: -15.40
- Trump 15-point plan Pakistan: -14.78
- Olmeca refinery Mexico: -14.10
- NCTC agency director quit: -12.92
- Bernie Sanders No Kings: -12.92

## Key Lessons Learned
1. READ QUESTIONS EXTREMELY CAREFULLY - many losses from misunderstanding what was asked
2. Exact naming matters (Bosnia-Herzegovina vs Bosnia and Herzegovina, Turkey vs Turkiye)
3. This sim picks unexpected/lesser-known answers very frequently
4. Location questions for Iran conflict almost always picked unexpected cities (Minab, Qazvin, Bushehr, Parand, Qom, Tabriz)
5. Sports venues can be neutral sites (Eritrea in Meknes, Morocco)
6. Bill names tend to be punchy acronyms (BETS OFF Act)
7. Lesser-known organizations often answer rescue/NGO questions (Emergency, Iranian Red Crescent)
8. Never assign >0.5 to any single outcome unless very strong evidence
9. Spread probability broadly for maximum expected value
10. Time-weighted scoring means early predictions matter most

blocks.text:
The simulation is now complete with all 331 questions resolved. Here's a summary of the final performance:

**Final Results:**
- **Accuracy:** 11.5% (38/331 questions with correct top prediction)
- **Brier Skill Score:** -0.021 (slightly worse than random baseline)
- **TW-Score:** -869.35

**Key takeaways:**
- The strongest wins came from correctly predicting Japan for AFC Women's Asian Cup (+30.86), Iran for Australia's visa ban (+30.94), and Jerusalem's Old City for the explosion (+28.52)
- The biggest losses came from misreading questions (Germany/Strait of Hormuz at -18.09), overconfident wrong predictions (Eritrea venue at -26.32), and the sim consistently choosing unexpected/lesser-known answers for location-specific questions
- Spreading probability across 5 diverse candidates was the right strategy for uncertain questions, but even broader coverage would have helped
- The Iran-Israel conflict dominated the question set, and specific location/name answers were consistently unpredictable
